decisions.title: AI Interpretation Task Creation and Notification
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run must create a background task in the task list, similar to the CC platform, and send a notification upon completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

decisions.title: AI Interpretation Notification Mechanism Details Pending
decisions.detail: The specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are pending confirmation in a requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: AI Interpretation Image Sources
decisions.detail: The system must support two image sources for AI interpretation: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

decisions.title: Suspected Point Confirmation and Monitoring
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation completes, a list of suspected points is generated. Users can confirm these points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: Monitoring Point Requirements: Reason and Polygon Upload
decisions.detail: When placing a point under monitoring, the user must provide a reason and upload a polygon. Large-area polygons are valid, while overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Image Upload Duplicate Prevention
decisions.detail: A mechanism is required to prevent duplicate image uploads due to large file sizes.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Image Comparison Feature: Manual Annotation
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull from the image library; users will annotate this manually instead.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: On-site Workstation for Model Execution
decisions.detail: A workstation will be set up on-site, and images will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which will then call the workstation to run the model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: GIS Provider for Base Map
decisions.detail: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

decisions.title: Map Viewer Imagery and Filtering Capabilities
decisions.detail: The map viewer must be able to load historically uploaded imagery and support filtering by date and image-name keyword (types: satellite, aerial photo, drone), displaying points on the map.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map Viewer 'Locate' Button and Zoom
decisions.detail: When a point is opened, a 'locate' button should be displayed, and the map must automatically zoom to that location.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Map Viewer Imagery Reversion to Point Display
decisions.detail: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery must automatically revert to point display.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Map Comparison Mode Lock and Release
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, the map view must be locked and panning disabled. A 'release drag mode' button must be provided to restore panning.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Map Viewer Image Transparency Adjustment
decisions.detail: Users must be able to adjust image transparency, which is an important feature for orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map Viewer Design Reference
decisions.detail: The map viewer's design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Suspected Point Project Organization by Year
decisions.detail: Projects are organized by year, and suspected points can be created within a project.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Suspected Point Layer Split by County/City Pending
decisions.detail: The decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected Point Polygon Requirement
decisions.detail: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Suspected Point Layers Organized by Year
decisions.detail: Each year forms one layer for suspected points.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data Interface Requirement
decisions.detail: A 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added. It should include fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13. Other fields include: survey serial number, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

[Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

decisions.title: Legacy MIS System Migration and Redesign
decisions.detail: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system. The interface will be redesigned, but its core functionality must remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: MIS Migration for Information Security Compliance
decisions.detail: The primary reason for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

decisions.title: MIS Migration Details Pending Confirmation
decisions.detail: Details regarding the legacy MIS system migration are pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Legacy System Follow-up/Reminder Feature
decisions.detail: The legacy system already includes a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily using email notifications.

evidence.quote: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

decisions.title: New System Follow-up Module Integration
decisions.detail: Items for follow-up can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the new system's follow-up module.

evidence.quote: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

decisions.title: New System Schedule-Tracking Interface
decisions.detail: The new system's follow-up feature must include a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining).

evidence.quote: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

decisions.title: New System Follow-up Dispatch Interface
decisions.detail: A follow-up dispatch interface is required, for which Shao-Chien already has a first draft.

evidence.quote: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

decisions.title: Follow-up Connection to Survey Report Management
decisions.detail: After follow-up, the system must connect to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: Data Flow Document Requirement
decisions.detail: A-Bao requires a data-flow document explaining the movement of data after AI interpretation, including data sources and where computations occur.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: AI Interpretation Data Flow Architecture
decisions.detail: The data flow for AI interpretation covers: image source -> interpretation task execution (workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database.

evidence.quote: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: Project Kick-off Expectation
decisions.detail: The project is currently in the bidding process and is expected to kick off in mid-May if things proceed smoothly.

evidence.quote: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

decisions.title: Immediate Requirements Interview Post-Bid Award

implementation_scope: Implement AI interpretation task creation as a background task in a task list, similar to the CC platform (D001).
Develop a notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (D001).
Integrate support for two image sources for AI interpretation: large-scale batch from Vendor A and individual user uploads (D003).
Develop user interface and backend logic for confirming suspected points (yes/no) after AI interpretation (D004).
Develop user interface and backend logic for placing suspected points under watch/monitoring (D004).
Implement functionality to capture a reason and upload a polygon when placing a point under monitoring (D005).
Implement automatic filtering for overly small polygons during monitoring point creation (D005).
Develop a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads, accounting for large file sizes (D006).
Adjust the two-period image comparison feature to support manual annotation by users (D007).
Implement map viewer capabilities to load historically uploaded imagery (D010).
Implement filtering for map viewer imagery by date and image-name keyword (satellite, aerial photo, drone) (D010).
Display points on the map viewer based on imagery (D010).
Develop a 'locate' button for points, which automatically zooms the map to the point's location (D011).
Implement automatic imagery reversion to point display when the map is zoomed out past a certain scale (D012).
Implement comparison mode for the map viewer, including locking the map view, disabling panning, and providing a 'release drag mode' button (D013).
Enable users to adjust image transparency within the map viewer (D014).
Implement project organization by year for suspected points (D016).
Enable creation of suspected points within a project (D016).
Implement requirement for uploading a polygon for suspected points (no image upload) (D018).
Organize suspected points into yearly layers (D019).

Add a 'New Survey Point Data' interface with fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key-monitoring-zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location (D020).
Migrate the original MS file management system to the new system, redesigning the interface while retaining core functionality (D021).
Integrate selection of suspected points into the new system's follow-up module (D025).
Develop a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) within the follow-up module (D026).
Develop a follow-up dispatch interface (D027).
Implement connection from the follow-up module to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images (D028).
Prepare a data-flow document explaining data movement after AI interpretation, including sources and computation locations (D029).

constraints.text: An on-site workstation will be used for model execution; images are sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which then calls the workstation to run the model (D008).
The base map must be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer (D009).
The map viewer's design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
The legacy MIS system migration must meet information-security compliance requirements (D022).
The AI interpretation data flow architecture is fixed: image source -> interpretation task execution (workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database (D030).

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull from the image library; users will annotate this manually instead (D007).
The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with its interface redesigned but core functionality remaining unchanged (D021).

open_technical_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism? (D002)
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city? (D017)
What are the interface design details for the MIS migration? (D023)
What are the pending system architecture decisions that need to be discussed? (D037)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, When the run starts, Then a background task is created in the task list.
Given an AI interpretation run is completed successfully, When the run finishes, Then a notification is sent upon completion.
Given a user wants to initiate an AI interpretation, When selecting image sources, Then the system allows selection of 'Vendor A nationwide batch'.
Given a user wants to initiate an AI interpretation, When selecting image sources, Then the system allows selection of 'individual images uploaded by the user'.
Given an AI interpretation has completed and generated a list of suspected points, When a user views a suspected point, Then they can choose to confirm it ('Yes').
Given an AI interpretation has completed and generated a list of suspected points, When a user views a suspected point, Then they can choose to reject it ('No').
Given a user has confirmed a suspected point, When they decide to monitor it, Then the system allows placing the point under watch/monitoring.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When they submit the monitoring request, Then a 'reason' must be provided.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When they submit the monitoring request, Then a polygon must be uploaded.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring and uploads a large-area polygon, When the system processes the polygon, Then the polygon is accepted.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring and uploads an overly small-area polygon, When the system processes the polygon, Then the polygon is automatically filtered out.
Given an image file has been previously uploaded, When the user attempts to upload the exact same image file again, Then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When historical imagery exists, Then the map viewer loads and displays historically uploaded imagery.
Given the map viewer is displaying a list of imagery, When the user filters by date, Then the list of imagery updates to show only images within the specified date range.
Given the map viewer is displaying a list of imagery, When the user filters by image-name keyword, Then the list of imagery updates to show only images matching the keyword.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When images have associated points, Then these points are displayed on the map.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the user filters by image type 'satellite', Then only satellite images are displayed.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the user filters by image type 'aerial photo', Then only aerial photo images are displayed.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the user filters by image type 'drone', Then only drone images are displayed.
Given a suspected point is opened in the system, When the point details are displayed, Then a 'locate' button is visible.
Given a user clicks the 'locate' button for an opened point, When the map viewer is active, Then the map automatically zooms to the location of that point.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the user zooms out past a defined scale threshold, Then the displayed imagery automatically reverts to point display.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When a user attempts to pan or drag the map, Then the map view is locked and panning is disabled.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When the user views the interface, Then a 'release drag mode' button is visible.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When the user clicks the 'release drag mode' button, Then panning is re-enabled.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When a user accesses transparency controls, Then they can adjust the transparency of the images.
Given a user adjusts image transparency, When the adjustment is applied, Then the displayed imagery visually reflects the new transparency level.
Given a user is managing suspected points, When creating a new project, Then the project must be associated with a specific year.
Given a project exists, When a user creates a suspected point, Then the suspected point is created within that project.
Given a user creates a suspected point, When submitting the point, Then a polygon must be uploaded.
Given a user creates a suspected point, When submitting the point, Then no image upload is required or allowed.
Given suspected points exist for multiple years, When viewing layers of suspected points, Then each year's suspected points form a distinct layer.

Given a user navigates to the 'New Survey Point Data' interface, When the interface loads, Then it displays fields for: drawing number, key-monitoring-zone, survey serial number, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
Given the migrated MIS system, When a user performs any core functionality, Then the outcome of that functionality is identical to the legacy system.
Given the migrated MIS system, When a user interacts with the interface, Then the interface design is updated.
Given a list of suspected points, When a user selects one or more points for follow-up, Then the selected points are successfully integrated into the new system's follow-up module.
Given a user views the follow-up module, When items are present for follow-up, Then a schedule-tracking interface is displayed.
Given an item is in the follow-up module with a defined due date, When viewing the schedule-tracking interface, Then it displays tracking information (e.g., 'days remaining') for that item.
Given a user needs to manage follow-up tasks, When navigating to the follow-up section, Then a follow-up dispatch interface is accessible.
Given a follow-up task is completed, When the user records the follow-up action, Then the system connects to survey-report management.
Given a follow-up action is recorded in survey-report management, When viewing the report, Then it includes fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
test_scenarios: Verify a task appears in the task list immediately after initiating an AI interpretation run.
Verify a completion notification is received after an AI interpretation finishes successfully.
Verify a failure notification is received if an AI interpretation fails.
Verify a user can successfully initiate an AI interpretation using the 'Vendor A nationwide batch' source.
Verify a user can successfully initiate an AI interpretation by uploading individual images.
Verify a user can confirm a suspected point and place it under monitoring.
Verify a user can reject a suspected point.
Verify a user cannot place a point under monitoring without providing a reason.
Verify a user cannot place a point under monitoring without uploading a polygon.
Verify a user can successfully place a point under monitoring with a valid reason and a large-area polygon.
Verify uploading the same image file twice results in a duplicate prevention message or action.
Verify uploading two different image files (even with similar names) is successful.
Verify a user can manually annotate comparison areas in the two-period image comparison feature.
Verify the 'pull from image library' option is absent or disabled in the two-period image comparison feature.
Verify the map viewer loads historical imagery correctly.
Verify filtering imagery by a specific date range works as expected.
Verify filtering imagery by keywords works.
Verify points associated with loaded imagery are correctly displayed on the map.
Verify filtering imagery by each image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) works independently.
Verify clicking 'locate' on a point zooms the map to its coordinates.
Verify zooming out past the predefined scale threshold switches the display from imagery to points.
Verify zooming in past the predefined scale threshold switches the display from points back to imagery.
Verify panning is disabled when comparison mode is active.
Verify clicking 'release drag mode' re-enables panning.
Verify adjusting image transparency from 0% to 100% and observing the visual change.
Verify projects can only be created with a year association.
Verify suspected points can be created successfully within an existing project.
Verify a user cannot create a suspected point without uploading a polygon.
Verify a user can successfully create a suspected point by uploading a polygon and no image.
Verify the interface does not prompt for image upload when creating a suspected point.
Verify that points created in different years appear in distinct layers in the relevant management interface.
Verify all specified fields are present and correctly labeled in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Verify data can be entered and saved for each field in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Perform a comprehensive set of actions in the new MIS interface and compare results against the legacy system's core functionality.
Verify the visual appearance and layout of the MIS interface are redesigned compared to the legacy system.
Verify all data records from the legacy system are successfully migrated and accessible in the new system.
Verify a single suspected point can be selected and added to the follow-up module.
Verify multiple suspected points can be selected and added to the follow-up module.
Verify the schedule-tracking interface is visible in the follow-up module.
Verify 'days remaining' (or equivalent) is correctly calculated and displayed for follow-up items with due dates.
Verify the 'follow-up dispatch interface' can be accessed.
Verify that completing a follow-up task correctly initiates the connection to survey-report management.
Verify that a survey report generated from a follow-up task contains fields for time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
Verify images can be uploaded and successfully associated with a survey report.
edge_cases: Test initiating multiple AI interpretation runs concurrently and observe task list behavior.
Test AI interpretation runs that take an extremely long time or complete very quickly.
Test initiating AI interpretation with a very large number of individual images.
Test initiating AI interpretation with invalid image file types (for user uploads).
Test confirming/rejecting all points in a very large list of suspected points.
Test confirming/rejecting points with varying data complexities (e.g., missing data, malformed data).
Test various polygon sizes for monitoring points, including just above/below the 'overly small' threshold.
Test with different valid reasons for monitoring points (long, short, special characters).
Test with invalid polygon formats/data (if the system allows upload before validation).
Test attempting to submit monitoring points with an empty reason or empty polygon file.
Test duplicate image prevention with very large image files.
Test duplicate image prevention with images that have minor metadata changes but identical pixel data, or identical pixel data but different file names.
Test concurrent uploads of the same image by different users (if multi-user system).
Test manual annotation with complex shapes, very small/large areas, and interactions with map zooming/panning.
Test filtering imagery with invalid date ranges (e.g., end date before start date).
Test filtering imagery with keywords that yield no results, very long keywords, or special character keywords.
Test filtering by multiple image types concurrently.
Test 'locate' on points with coordinates far away from the current map view, or with invalid/missing coordinate data.
Test rapidly zooming in and out across the imagery/point display threshold.
Test switching in and out of map comparison mode multiple times.
Test attempting to pan using various input methods (mouse, touch) while comparison mode is locked.
Test image transparency adjustment with multiple layers of imagery (if applicable) or with very dark/light imagery.
Test creating projects for past, current, and future years.
Test creating a very large number of suspected points within one project.
Test creating suspected points with empty or invalid polygon file uploads.
Test with maximum length inputs for text fields in 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Test with invalid date formats, non-numeric values for numeric fields (volume, area, coordinates) in 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Test entering coordinates that are outside of expected ranges in 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
Test any known edge cases or rare workflows from the legacy MIS system after migration.
Test selecting an already followed-up point for follow-up.
Test selecting all suspected points for follow-up, or points from different projects/years.
Test schedule-tracking with follow-up items due today, tomorrow, next week, and overdue, or without a defined due date.
Test follow-up report creation with very long descriptions or action taken notes.
Test with multiple image uploads for a single follow-up report.
Test attempting to save a follow-up report with missing required fields (if any).
unresolved_behaviors: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., delivery method, content, recipient) need to be confirmed.
What defines an 'overly small area' for a polygon when placing a point under monitoring? (e.g., minimum square meters/pixels, criteria for filtering)
What is the specific 'certain scale' threshold at which map imagery reverts to point display?
What is the expected range of image transparency adjustment (e.g., 0-100%) and the granularity of control (e.g., slider, numeric input)?
Needs confirmation on whether suspected-point layers should be split by county/city. If so, what are the specific criteria for splitting and how is this reflected in the UI?
Specific interface design details for the MIS migration and a clear definition of 'core functionality' that must remain unchanged need to be confirmed.
What specific schedule-tracking metrics (e.g., 'days remaining', status, progress) should be displayed in the follow-up module, and how are due dates defined for follow-up items?
What are the specific functionalities and expected behaviors of the 'follow-up dispatch interface'?
Who are the intended recipients for follow-up notifications, and what is the schedule/frequency for these notifications?

decision_history: It was decided that each AI interpretation run must create a background task in the task list (similar to the CC platform) and send a notification upon completion.
The system must support two image sources for AI interpretation: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, and individual user uploads.
After AI interpretation, a list of suspected points is generated, which users can confirm (yes/no) and decide to place under watch/monitoring.
When placing a point under monitoring, users must provide a reason and upload a polygon, with large-area polygons being valid and overly small areas filtered out.
A mechanism is required to prevent duplicate image uploads due to their large file sizes.
The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull from the image library; instead, users will annotate this manually.
The map viewer must be able to load historically uploaded imagery, filter by date and image-name keyword (satellite, aerial photo, drone), and display points on the map.
A 'locate' button should display when a point is opened, automatically zooming the map to that location.
When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery must automatically revert to point display.
In comparison mode, the map view must be locked with panning disabled; a 'release drag mode' button will restore panning.
Users must be able to adjust image transparency, which is critical for orthophoto analysis.
Projects are organized by year, allowing suspected points to be created within a project, with each year forming one layer for suspected points.
Suspected points require an uploaded polygon, but no image upload is needed.

A 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added, including fields like drawing number, key-monitoring-zone, survey serial number, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, coordinates (X/Y, longitude/latitude), and approximate location.

The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with its interface redesigned but core functionality remaining unchanged. This migration is primarily to meet information-security compliance requirements.

The new system will integrate a follow-up module where items can be selected from suspected points. It must include a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., days remaining) and a dispatch interface (Shao-Chien has a draft).

Post-follow-up, the system must connect to survey-report management, recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images, leveraging the legacy system's email-based follow-up/reminder functionality.
dependencies: AI model execution relies on an on-site workstation set up and called by the client's Access Point (AP).
Base map functionality relies on GIS Provider B providing data via GeoServer.
Map viewer design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The requirements interview (scheduled for 2026-05-20) is dependent on preparation work by Sherry: consolidating existing interfaces, preparing a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram.
A-Bao's review of the AI interpretation data flow is dependent on Sherry preparing the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) by 2026-05-18.
MIS migration interface design details are pending and will impact the design and development teams.
risks: Immediate Requirements Interview Post-Bid Award: There is a risk that a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately on the afternoon the bid is awarded. This implies a compressed timeline for preparation and stakeholder availability. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High on initial project setup and planning).
alignment_gaps: Specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are pending confirmation in a requirements interview, indicating a potential gap in stakeholder expectations or detailed requirements.
The decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation, representing a potential divergence in data organization expectations.
Interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration are pending confirmation, which could lead to rework if not aligned early.
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule needs confirmation, which could impact user experience and compliance if not clearly defined and agreed upon.
open_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism? (Owned by PM/QA)
Should suspected-point layers be split by county/city for data organization? (Owned by PM/QA)
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? (Owned by PM/QA)
Who will receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule? (Owned by PM/QA)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May (if bidding proceeds smoothly).
Sherry to finish the 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review by 2026-05-18.
Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15, planned topic: Discuss system architecture.
Sherry to organize existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) for use in the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.

system_requirements: The system SHALL create a background task for each AI interpretation run within the task list, similar to the CC platform.
The system SHALL send a notification upon the completion of an AI interpretation task.
The system SHALL support image ingestion from a one-time large-scale nationwide batch provided by Vendor A for AI interpretation.
The system SHALL support individual user-uploaded images as a source for AI interpretation.
The system SHALL implement a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads, accounting for large file sizes.
The system SHALL generate a list of suspected points upon completion of AI interpretation.
The system SHALL allow users to confirm or reject suspected points from the generated list.
The system SHALL allow users to designate confirmed suspected points for watch/monitoring.
The system SHALL require a reason to be provided by the user when placing a suspected point under monitoring.
The system SHALL require a polygon to be uploaded by the user when placing a suspected point under monitoring.
The system SHALL accept large-area polygons for monitoring points.
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygons provided for monitoring points.
The map viewer SHALL load historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery by date.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery by image-name keyword.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering of imagery by type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).
The map viewer SHALL display identified points on the map.
The map viewer SHALL display a 'locate' button when a point is opened.
The map viewer SHALL automatically zoom to the location of an opened point.
The map viewer SHALL automatically revert from imagery display to point display when zoomed out beyond a predefined scale.
In comparison mode, the map viewer SHALL lock the map view and disable panning.
The map viewer SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning functionality when in comparison mode.
The map viewer SHALL allow users to adjust image transparency.
The system SHALL organize suspected points within projects, where projects are organized by year.
The system SHALL allow creation of suspected points within an existing project.
The system SHALL represent suspected points for each year as a distinct layer.
The system SHALL require an uploaded polygon for the creation of a suspected point.
The system MUST NOT require an image upload for the creation of a suspected point.
The system SHALL provide a 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
The core functionality of the migrated MS file management system SHALL remain unchanged in the new system.
The user interface for the migrated MS file management system SHALL be redesigned as part of the new system development.
The new system, including the migrated MIS functionality, SHALL comply with relevant information security requirements (inferred - needs confirmation).
The new system SHALL allow users to select suspected points for inclusion in the follow-up module.
The new system's follow-up module SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface, displaying metrics such as days remaining.
The new system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface.
The system SHALL integrate follow-up actions with survey-report management.
The integration with survey-report management SHALL include recording of follow-up time, description, action taken, and associated uploaded images.
data_flows: Images -> Client's Access Point (AP) -> On-site Workstation (for AI model execution) (D008, D030)
Base Map Data (GIS Provider B) -> GeoServer -> Map Viewer (inferred from D009)
Image Source -> AI Interpretation Task Execution (On-site Workstation) -> AI Result Output -> Monitoring Database (D030)
Follow-up Module -> Survey-Report Management (data: time, description, action taken, uploaded images) (D028)
interface_contracts: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (enum: precise coordinates, aerial survey, rapid monitoring, unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location. (D020)
The follow-up dispatch interface design SHALL leverage the existing first draft provided by Shao-Chien. (D027)
The 'add suspected point' data interface SHALL be completed by 2026-05-18, referencing manual figure 2.13. (D033)

constraints.text: The AI interpretation model execution SHALL occur on an on-site workstation.
The base map SHALL be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer's user interface and experience design SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The migration of the legacy MIS system is mandated to achieve information-security compliance requirements.
The AI interpretation data flow SHALL adhere to the sequence: image source -> interpretation task execution (on workstation) -> result output -> monitoring database.
A requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to occur immediately on the day the project bid is awarded, implying a tight turnaround for initial SA deliverables.

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature SHALL rely on manual user annotation instead of pulling from the system's image library.
The legacy MS file management system SHALL be migrated to the new system.

open_analysis_questions: Clarify the specific details and technical mechanism of the AI interpretation task completion notification, including protocols, data payload, and target endpoints.
Determine whether suspected point data layers SHALL be logically or physically partitioned by county/city, and the implications for data access and querying.
Confirm the detailed interface design specifications and migration strategy for the legacy MIS system to the new platform.
Define the system's capabilities for configurable recipients and schedules for follow-up notifications, including the underlying data model to support this functionality.

stakeholder.business_summary: We are developing a new system to automatically identify and monitor changes using artificial intelligence (AI) on imagery. This system will improve our ability to track potential issues and enhance overall data security by upgrading our old file management system. We anticipate the project officially starting in mid-May.
key_impact: Users will gain a powerful tool to quickly identify potential issues from images using AI analysis.
The new system will provide better tools for monitoring identified points and managing follow-up actions.
Critical business data will be more secure and compliant through the migration of our outdated file management system.
Analysts will have enhanced map viewing capabilities to review historical imagery and compare different periods more effectively.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirmation is needed on the specific method for notifying users when AI image analysis tasks are completed.
We need to decide if detected points should be organized (or 'split') by county or city within the system.
Final approval is required on the detailed interface design for the migration of the legacy file management system.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the exact details for how users will be notified when AI image analysis tasks are finished?
Should the detected points be grouped by county or city for better organization and reporting?
What are the final design specifics for the new interface of the migrated file management system?
Who should receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule?

issues.description: The test scenario 'Verify a failure notification is received if an AI interpretation fails.' is a reasonable addition but extends beyond the explicit detail in D001, which only states 'a notification is sent when it completes' (implying successful completion).
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a source note for implied scenarios or ensure the acceptance criteria explicitly covers failure notifications if required.

issues.description: The test scenario 'Verify zooming in past the predefined scale threshold switches the display from points back to imagery.' is an inferred behavior, as D012 explicitly states imagery reverts to point display when zoomed *out* past a certain scale, but the reverse action is not explicitly mentioned.
issues.suggestion: Consider adding a source note for implied scenarios or verify if the reverse behavior (zooming in) is an explicit requirement from stakeholders.

issues.description: Decision D037, 'Next Meeting Topic: System Architecture', is categorized as an 'open_question' in the extracted decisions and included in the Engineer's 'open_technical_questions', but it is not explicitly listed in the PM's 'open_questions' or the Stakeholder's 'major_unresolved_questions'. While it's a discussion topic rather than a requirement detail, its absence from the higher-level open questions might be an oversight if it's considered a significant architectural decision still to be made.
issues.suggestion: Consider including D037 in the PM and Stakeholder views' open questions or alignment gaps, perhaps rephrased to reflect its nature as a pending architectural discussion that needs resolution.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are well-structured and accurately reflect the extracted decisions. Hallucination checks passed, with minor 'info' level observations where the QA view inferred logical but unstated test cases. Quality checks show the outputs are useful and ready for their audience. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected. The minor issues do not block publishing.

raw_notes:
# Work Meeting Minutes

**Project:** Illegal Excavation AI — Work Item Discussion
**Date:** 2026-05-04
**Attendees:** A-Bao, Ming-Xian, Sherry
**Minutes by:** Sherry

## Topics for This Session

- Confirming the functional scope of each Illegal-Excavation-AI work item
- Migration plan for the legacy MIS system
- AI interpretation task workflow and data flow explanation
- Map viewer features and image-comparison mode discussion
- Follow-up/reminder module planning

## Decisions

### [AI Interpretation Work Item]

- Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes
- Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
- Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user
- After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring
- When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)
- Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

### [Image Comparison Feature Adjustment]

- Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead
- A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model
- The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

### [Map Viewer Features]

- The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map
- Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location
- When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display
- In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning
- Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)
- The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

### [Suspected Point Management]

- Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project
- Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview
- Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed
- Each year forms one layer
- **Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)**

  *[Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]*

### [Legacy MIS System Migration]

- The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged
- The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements
- Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

### [Follow-up/Reminder Feature]

- The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification
- Planning for the new system's follow-up feature:
  - Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module
  - Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)
  - Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)
  - After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

### [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

- A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
- Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database

### [Project Timeline]

- The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May
- On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

## Action Items

| Owner | Task | Due |
|---|---|---|
| Sherry | Finish the "add suspected point" data interface (refer to figure 2.13) | Before 5/18 |
| Sherry | Organize existing interfaces (including the parts Shao-Chien owns) for use in the requirements interview | Before 5/20 |
| Sherry | Prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review | Before 5/18 |

## Next Meeting

**Time:** 2026-05-15
**Planned topic:** Discuss system architecture

## Requirements Interview

**Time:** 2026-05-20
**Planned topic:** First requirements interview
**Prep needed:** Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

**Open items to confirm:**
- Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism
- Whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city
- Interface design details for the MIS migration
- Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule
